topic: Anthropic Says New Claude Mythos AI Is Too Risky for Public Use

caption.hook: Anthropic reportedly says its new Claude AI is too risky to release. 🧵
caption.summary: According to the report, Anthropic isn't shipping its newest Claude model to the public — they're saying it's too risky for now. Take the specifics with a grain of salt until there's official confirmation.
caption.content:
The bigger picture (true either way):
• AI capability is moving faster than the rules around it.
• Whether a model ships often comes down to safety, not just how smart it is.
• "Too risky to release" is becoming a real sentence labs say out loud.

What actually helps you: don't wait for the next model. Get genuinely good with the AI tools you already have. Skill beats access.
caption.cta: Save this + follow @genzcapital for daily AI tools & how-tos. No fluff. No hype.

slides.headline: The claim: too risky to release
slides.body: The report says Anthropic is holding its newest Claude back from the public over safety. The exact details aren't confirmed yet — treat them as a claim, not gospel.

slides.headline: Capability is outrunning the rules
slides.body: True regardless of the specifics: AI is getting more powerful faster than anyone's figuring out how to release it safely.

slides.headline: Shipping now depends on safety
slides.body: Whether a model reaches the public increasingly comes down to whether a lab thinks it's safe — not just how smart it is.

slides.headline: Access is not your edge
slides.body: Everyone gets powerful models eventually. The people who win are the ones who actually know how to use them.

slides.headline: What you just learned
points: A report says Anthropic is holding its newest Claude back over safety.
Details aren't confirmed — treat the specifics as a claim, not fact.
Either way: capability is outrunning the rules around releasing it.
Your edge is skill with today's tools, not access to tomorrow's.
